items.headline: Major electrical upgrade permitted for Porter Drive R&D facility
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development operations. The work includes new transformers, switches, and conduit extending to a substation on Hanover Drive, indicating substantial expansion of power capacity for the building's tenants.

items.headline: Historic house preservation weighed against four-townhome development
items.summary: A project at 434 Crescent Avenue would retain a 960-square-foot historic single-family home while adding four three-story townhomes in the rear. The Heritage Preservation Commission reviewed the resource alteration permit on May 6 to determine if the development adequately protects the historic resource.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal immigration enforcement
items.summary: The city council passed a resolution prohibiting city-owned and operated property from being used for federal civil immigration enforcement actions. This policy protects residents by preventing federal immigration authorities from using city facilities for deportation or enforcement operations.

items.headline: City expands digital signage agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: San José amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects the visual landscape of public spaces and could generate revenue or advertising presence around the city.

items.headline: San José seeks new encampment cleanup service providers
items.summary: The city released a request for proposals for encampment cleanup services on May 5th. This indicates an ongoing effort to manage homelessness through sanitation and site management.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: City Council took actions related to the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, though specific details are limited. This infrastructure project likely affects transportation at a key city facility.

items.headline: City negotiating purchase of Finch Avenue property with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District over the purchase price and terms for 10480 Finch Avenue. The outcome could affect future development or use of this property in the city.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide development of recreational spaces in that area. This plan could reshape how residents access green space and trails in one of Milpitas's key development zones.
